gene_region.label: ERBB2 transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical ERBB2 genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window used by this app to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval begins.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: ERBB2 encodes HER2/ERBB2, an ErbB family receptor tyrosine kinase dimerization partner. This bundle emphasizes HER2 receptor dimer signaling, MAPK/PI3K activation, amplification biology, and anti-HER2 therapy response.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local ERBB2 bundle is HER2 receptor signaling oriented. Interpret markers through exact allele class, zygosity, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context rather than as stand-alone deterministic calls.
variant_effect_overview: ERBB2 variants can alter or tag HER2 receptor dimer signaling, MAPK/PI3K activation, amplification biology, and anti-HER2 therapy response; effect direction depends on allele class, tissue, dosage, and biological context.
Common markers, pharmacogenetic alleles, structural events, and high-impact pathogenic variants should be kept separate during interpretation.
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: ERBB2 methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a HER2 receptor dimer signaling, MAPK/PI3K activation, amplification biology, and anti-HER2 therapy response gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for ERBB2.
Because ERBB2 biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.
Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against HER2 receptor dimer signaling, MAPK/PI3K activation, amplification biology, and anti-HER2 therapy response.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use ERBB2 promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for HER2 receptor dimer signaling, MAPK/PI3K activation, amplification biology, and anti-HER2 therapy response.

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000141736: ERBB2 coordinate source

biorender_visuals.label: ERBB2 biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: ERBB2: HER2 receptor dimer signaling, MAPK/PI3K activation, amplification biology, and anti-HER2 therapy response
biorender_visuals.template_title: Intracellular Layout - Receptor Signaling from Plasma Membrane to Nucleus (2)
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a ERBB2 HER2 receptor signaling thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to HER2 receptor dimer signaling, MAPK/PI3K activation, amplification biology, and anti-HER2 therapy response and HER2-amplified oncology context, ErbB receptor signaling. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support.

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: ERBB2 amplification model is curated as a ERBB2 marker for HER2-amplified oncology context, ErbB receptor signaling. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the HER2 receptor dimer signaling, MAPK/PI3K activation, amplification biology, and anti-HER2 therapy response pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Curated research and clinical-context marker; external clinical classification is required for diagnostic use.
functional_effects: Can modify or tag HER2 receptor dimer signaling, MAPK/PI3K activation, amplification biology, and anti-HER2 therapy response.
Best interpreted with tissue, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context.
research_context: Use this marker as a ERBB2 pathway-context signal, not as a stand-alone diagnosis.
Sequence, methylation, expression, and protein data may point in different directions and should be reconciled.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Pair ERBB2 amplification model with ERBB2 methylation only as local regulatory context; methylation does not prove the variant's biochemical effect.
